title: Rich Croatian Pepper Stew
description: A sour and velvety Croatian dish featuring mixed peppers, perfect for a date night, made with low sodium ingredients and cooked to perfection in a cazuela, using sous vide for tender results.
instructions: Preheat the sous vide machine to 180 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the mixed peppers with a little bit of oil and season with paprika.
Place the peppers in a single layer in the cazuela and cook in the sous vide machine for 2 hours.
In a large skillet, saute the chopped onion and minced garlic until softened.
Add the tomato paste to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the low sodium chicken broth to the skillet and bring to a simmer.
Remove the peppers from the sous vide machine and add to the skillet.
Stir in the sour cream and cook until heated through.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley and serve hot.
Cook poultry to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F before serving.
